stimulus: Initially credited to Frans Hals, the celebrated Dutch Golden Age portraitist, the 1633 canvas Boy with a Flute attracted renewed scrutiny in the 2010s when conservators determined that the painting—which portrays a smiling youth tuning his instrument—was in fact produced by obscure Haarlem genre ______ Judith Leyster (1609–1660).
stem: Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?
rationale:
Choice A is the best answer. The convention being tested is the use of punctuation between a descriptive title and a proper noun. No punctuation is needed to set off the proper noun "Judith Leyster" from the descriptive title that precedes it, "obscure Haarlem genre painter."

Choice B is incorrect because no punctuation is needed. Choice C is incorrect because no punctuation is needed. Choice D is incorrect because no punctuation is needed.
